body.title: Add trace_record() + debug_mode flag to DiagnosticBase
body.result: trace_record(event, data), debug_mode, last_traces(n, since) added to DiagnosticBase; prune covers trace/*.jsonl; 6 new tests (31 total passing)
body.description:
DiagnosticBase already has a tagged logger and dump(). Add structured trace support so any rack device gets queryable history out of the box.

Add to base.py:
- `trace_record(event: str, data: dict = None)` — writes a JSON-per-record entry to datacenter_logs/<device>/trace/<YYYYMMDD>.jsonl. Each record: {ts, device, event, data}.
- `debug_mode: bool = False` flag — when True, trace records are also emitted via the loguru logger at DEBUG level in real time.
- `last_traces(n: int = 20, since: datetime = None) -> list[dict]` — reads from the trace log files and returns N most recent records, optionally filtered by timestamp.
- Prune: extend the existing 30-day retention to cover trace/ subdirectory (same schedule as current log pruning).

**Affected files:** /home/akien/dev/src/UnseenUniversity/diagnostic_base/base.py, /home/akien/dev/src/UnseenUniversity/tests/test_diagnostic_base.py (extend existing or create)
**Design rules:** OOP-first; loguru logger; JSON-per-record logs; datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ hierarchy (CLAUDE.md)
**Scope boundary:** DiagnosticBase base class only; NOT device-specific changes; NOT /diagnose skill (T-diagnose-skill); NOT shim.py
**Test plan:** trace_record() writes JSON entry to correct datacenter_logs/<device>/trace/ path; last_traces(n=5) returns 5 most recent; debug_mode=True causes trace to also appear in loguru output; 30-day prune removes old trace entries; device_name isolation (two devices don't share trace files)
